title: How to Integrate with Zeus
description: Use these guides to connect your tools, channels, and workflows to Zeus.

title: Build Plugins with Codex and Claude Code
description: A practical guide to getting coding assistants to generate a Zeus plugin scaffold you can ship.

title: Publish to the Zeus Plugin Registry
description: How to submit your plugin via pull request so Zeus users can discover and install it.

title: Zeus Plugins - API Reference
description: Endpoints for install/search/config, OAuth flows, server activation, channels, and custom webhooks.

title: Zeus Plugins - Channel Plugin Example
description: Server-side channel adapter plus custom webhook route for provider callbacks.

title: Zeus Plugins - Channel Runtime
description: How server plugins expose channels, custom webhooks, and outbound send operations.

title: Zeus Plugins - Daemon Plugin Example
description: Python client component with tools, hooks, command/service/CLI/provider/gateway/http operations, and OAuth declaration.

title: Zeus Plugins - Daemon Runtime
description: How Zeus loads and executes plugin-defined tools, operations, and hooks on the Mac daemon.

title: Zeus Plugins - Getting Started
description: Bare minimum files, manifest structure, and first install for Zeus plugins.

title: Zeus Plugins - Manifest Reference
description: Complete contract for zeus.plugin.json (Zeus Native Plugins).

title: Zeus Plugins - OpenClaw Differences
description: Zeus plugins are loosely inspired by OpenClaw concepts, but are Zeus-native and Python-first.

title: Zeus Plugins - Troubleshooting
description: Common plugin failures and fast fixes.
